claim_text: Across the benchmarks reported in the paper, the automatic evaluators matched oracle metrics or human judgments at rates ranging from 74.4% to 92.9%.
caveats: No direct contradiction is visible, but the spread from 74.4% to 92.9% shows performance varies materially by setting and model.
mechanism_or_bounds: No causal mechanism for this agreement range is established in the provided evidence; the result is bounded to the reported benchmark settings and evaluator-model combinations.
contradiction_note: No direct contradiction is visible, but the spread from 74.4% to 92.9% shows performance varies materially by setting and model.

claim_text: On WebArena, the modular Captioner plus GPT-4 evaluator slightly outperformed the end-to-end GPT-4V evaluator against the oracle evaluator, while the Captioner plus Mixtral variant performed worse than both.
caveats: No direct contradiction is visible, but the relative ranking on WebArena sits alongside stronger Android-in-the-Wild results for Mixtral, so performance does not appear uniform across domains.
mechanism_or_bounds: The provided evidence supports only the comparative outcome, not the cause; any explanation for why the modular GPT-4 setup did better than GPT-4V or Mixtral remains uncertain.
contradiction_note: No direct contradiction is visible, but the relative ranking on WebArena sits alongside stronger Android-in-the-Wild results for Mixtral, so performance does not appear uniform across domains.

claim_text: Using the evaluator as the reward signal in Reflexion improved the best-performing GPT-4 WebArena agent by up to 29% relative, with a 16% relative gain reported for the Captioner plus Mixtral evaluator.
caveats: Visible tension remains because the same evidence base also indicates evaluator noise matters asymmetrically, so improvement does not imply robust reward quality under all error types.
mechanism_or_bounds: A bounded mechanism is suggested rather than fully proven: evaluator feedback appears useful for iterative retry and refinement, but the reported gains are limited to the WebArena Reflexion setup and depend on evaluator error patterns.
contradiction_note: Visible tension remains because the same evidence base also indicates evaluator noise matters asymmetrically, so improvement does not imply robust reward quality under all error types.

claim_text: The paper suggests evaluator errors are more damaging when they falsely reject successful actions than when they falsely accept failures, because false negatives trigger unnecessary retries of already successful executions.
caveats: No direct contradiction is visible, but the support is preliminary, so the mechanism should be treated as suggestive rather than settled.
mechanism_or_bounds: The mechanism given in the paper is that a false negative forces a retry of a success, while a false positive mainly removes a retry opportunity; this is explicitly bounded by the paper's preliminary analysis rather than a definitive causal test.
contradiction_note: No direct contradiction is visible, but the support is preliminary, so the mechanism should be treated as suggestive rather than settled.
